title: Implement hold, human restoration, and approval audit

environment.os: Windows local; GitHub Actions Ubuntu and Windows pending

commands.name: Missing approval-authority domain
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/approval-authority.test.ts --project property tests/property/approval-authority.property.test.ts --reporter=dot
commands.expectedFailure: The focused suites failed to import the not-yet-created approval-authority module

commands.name: Missing approval-authority migration and PostgreSQL persistence
commands.expectedFailure: The migration inventory lacked 0015_approval_authority.sql and the PostgreSQL approval repository did not exist

commands.name: Missing authenticated approval API
commands.expectedFailure: The HTTP suite could not import the not-yet-created approval and decision routes

commands.name: Visibility loss lacked exact prior-visible and frozen-retry evidence
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/approval-authority.test.ts --reporter=dot
commands.expectedFailure: A boolean visibility claim could place approval on hold without a stored VERIFIED checkpoint and exact frozen retry lineage

commands.name: Undefined approval-authority browser behavior
commands.expectedFailure: The four approval scenarios had undefined browser and API steps before the DET-05 panel and step definitions existed

commands.name: Approval reducer, properties, persistence, and authenticated routes
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/approval-authority.test.ts --project property tests/property/approval-authority.property.test.ts --project integration tests/integration/approval-authority.test.ts apps/web/tests/integration/approval-authority-routes.test.ts --reporter=dot

commands.name: Approval hold, repair, visibility-loss, and human-restoration browser stories
Lint, repository type check, and optimized production build
commands.command: pnpm lint; pnpm typecheck; pnpm build

commands.name: Source-bound optimized-production approval-state capture
Evidence inventory recognized DET-05 proof
commands.command: pnpm evidence:check; pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/evidence-harness.test.ts --reporter=dot
commands.expectedFailure: Traceability accepted twenty-three manifests and 239 unique proof files while the explicit inventory regression still expected twenty-two manifests and 220 proof files

commands.name: DET-05 evidence traceability and explicit inventory
commands.command: pnpm evidence:check; pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/evidence-harness.test.ts --reporter=dot

commands.name: Complete repository verification recognized the new migration table
commands.expectedFailure: The generated migration report correctly contained 32 tables while the explicit unit regression still expected the previous 31-table inventory

commands.name: Unbounded integration workers saturated real browser fixtures
commands.expectedFailure: Two existing RUN-03 tests exhausted real browser resources when the expanded integration project started unbounded workers; both returned MODEL_REQUEST_FAILED and passed immediately in isolation

commands.name: Bounded complete integration suite
Complete production-browser matrix found cross-feature selector and state-cardinality regressions
commands.expectedFailure: The DET-05 current-state badge reused the software form's approval-state test identifier in 26 workflows, and one DET-03 assertion did not yet compare the six unique states after DET-05 added a second evidence-distinct NOT_VISIBLE finding

commands.name: Affected approval, finding, and inventory production-browser stories
Clean-checkout CI exposed stale role-gated approval UI during a session downgrade
commands.command: GitHub Actions CI run 29870880986 / Verify on ubuntu-latest
commands.expectedFailure: The existing AUT-02 test-operator BDD scenario observed GET /approval returning 403 because the client briefly rendered the approval panel with the previous privacy officer's roles after the signed session had changed

commands.name: Clean-checkout Windows CI exposed a browser lookup inside the scripted model transport
commands.command: GitHub Actions CI run 29870880986 / Verify on windows-latest
commands.expectedFailure: The existing RUN-03 popup-policy regression returned MODEL_REQUEST_FAILED when its scripted transport performed a live page lookup; the lower browser policy assertion never received the deterministic fixture action

commands.name: Role downgrade clears the prior principal's privileged panels
Static scripted action reaches the lower browser policy consistently
commands.command: Repeat 3x: pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/computer-use.test.ts --reporter=dot

commands.name: Complete evidence-bound optimized-production repository verification
Complete latest-main integration verification after DET-04 merge
Production dependency audit

scenarios.scenario: A witnessed conflict places one receipt-linked hold
A repaired rerun cannot restore approval automatically
Repeated visibility loss on a frozen retry places a hold
Only an authorized human can restore approval after reviewing a named rerun

assets.altText: Desktop Pactwire approval panel showing the fictional district record in APPROVED state before any finding is applied, with the imported human-owned source named.
assets.caption: The controlled record begins with the district's existing imported approval; Pactwire does not claim to have granted it.

assets.altText: Narrow Pactwire approval panel showing the fictional district's imported APPROVED state and its source without horizontal overflow.
assets.caption: The narrow layout preserves the approval source and authority boundary in reading order.

assets.altText: Desktop Pactwire approval panel after a stored witnessed conflict, showing HOLD, the WITNESSED_CONFLICT reason, and one exact contributing receipt.
assets.caption: Exact deterministic conflict evidence pauses the existing approval once and links the receipt that caused the hold.

assets.altText: Narrow Pactwire approval panel showing the receipt-linked APPROVED to HOLD transition and witnessed-conflict reason.
assets.caption: The narrow transition view keeps the reason, receipt, and automation limit visible.

assets.altText: Desktop Pactwire approval panel showing HOLD after the repaired named rerun and direct language that automation cannot restore approval.
assets.caption: Not re-observing the prior conflict in a named rerun leaves the existing hold unchanged for human review.

assets.altText: Narrow Pactwire approval panel showing that the repaired named rerun leaves approval on HOLD pending a person.
assets.caption: The narrow repair state does not imply safety or silently turn HOLD back into APPROVED.

assets.altText: Desktop Pactwire approval panel showing HOLD for required visibility loss after a previously verified checkpoint and exact frozen retry remained not visible.
assets.caption: Persistent required visibility loss has its own reason and evidence lineage; it is not labeled a contract conflict.

assets.altText: Narrow Pactwire approval panel showing the required-visibility-loss hold, affected checkpoint, and contributing receipt.
assets.caption: The narrow view preserves the retry-gated reason and checkpoint provenance.

assets.altText: Desktop Pactwire approval panel showing APPROVED after a fictional privacy officer signs a named-scope restoration reason, with the earlier automated hold retained in history.
assets.caption: Only the authorized human decision restores approval, and the append-only audit keeps both the original hold and restoration actor.

assets.altText: Narrow Pactwire approval panel showing the fictional human-signed restoration and retained approval history.
assets.caption: The narrow restored state still names the human owner, reason, named test scope, and prior hold history.

proofs.description: Strict approval snapshot, finding signal, receipt lineage, signed human decision, idempotent reducer, lock, and PostgreSQL repository
Append-only approval events, human decisions, receipt contributions, uniqueness, and immutable-row guards
Authenticated approval review and server-owned application of stored finding, receipt, run, and visibility evidence
Permission-gated, signed human approval decision endpoint with named-scope and clean-rerun requirements
Direct-language current state, evidence-linked hold reasons, restoration boundary, signed decision form, and append-only history UI
Authority decision table, exact receipt binding, visibility retry gate, repair behavior, human restoration, and idempotency examples
Fixed-seed PROP-02, PROP-03, and PROP-11 generated automation, concurrency, and append-only invariants
Existing fixed-seed PROP-01 proof that no automated event sequence can enter or restore APPROVED
PostgreSQL transaction, row-lock, duplicate receipt, immutable event, and human-decision persistence tests
Authenticated review, server-derived evidence, role denial, cross-workspace concealment, and human restoration route tests
Four real-browser stories covering receipt-linked conflict hold, repaired rerun, retry-gated visibility loss, and signed human restoration
Browser and authenticated API assertions plus source-bound desktop, narrow, before, and after capture
Controlled fictional approval, finding, receipt, visibility, and actor fixtures used across deterministic layers
Disposable PostgreSQL approval-authority fixture with transactional schema setup and teardown
Sanitized authenticated API snapshot before any automated hold
Sanitized API snapshot of the one-event receipt-linked witnessed-conflict hold
Sanitized API snapshot proving a repaired named rerun leaves the prior hold unchanged
Sanitized API snapshot of the retry-gated required-visibility-loss hold and checkpoint
Sanitized API snapshot retaining the automated hold and signed human restoration history
Curated DET-05 evidence description, reproduction commands, authority boundary, and explicit P1 deferral

knownLimitations: The committed proof uses fictional identities, a fictional product, reserved identifiers, synthetic observations, and a controlled agreement; it does not establish effectiveness on real school software.
A clean sampled rerun means only that the prior conflict was not re-observed in the named tests; it does not establish safety, compliance, approval, or behavior outside that scope.
Prior checkpoint visibility and frozen retry lineage are derived from stored controlled run manifests; external truth for agreement meaning and destination identity remains human-owned.
FR-056 P1 webhook or ticket delivery is explicitly deferred until delivery behavior and bounded-language copy have their own failing-first tests and evidence.
Production operational concerns such as notification integration, retention operations, and external district-system reconciliation remain outside DET-05.
GitHub Actions clean-checkout verification is pending for the DET-05 pull request.
